title: Typed Semantic Definitions Refactor
subtitle: One-off refactor living doc for making living-doc structure and behavior executable without moving concrete docs out of JSON.
scope: Living Doc Compositor registry, templates, MCP diagnostics, skills, generated docs, and contract tests.
sourceCoverage: Seeded from GitHub issue #163 and internal model pages created on 2026-05-05.
objective: Integrate typed semantic definitions into Living Doc Compositor so living-doc structure and behavior have one executable source that generates the registry/template artifacts, relationship graphs, MCP diagnostics, tests, and reference diagrams while concrete living doc instances remain JSON.
successCondition: The refactor is complete when typed definitions are the maintained executable source for the intended living-doc semantic machinery, generate the selected registry/template artifacts deterministically, preserve existing JSON/HTML rendering and sync flows, drive MCP relationship/stage diagnostics and valid operations, support skill use through concrete patchable operations, and have contract tests that prevent drift. Individual implementation slices are checkpoints toward this full integration, not alternative objectives or completion claims.

issueReferences.title: Add typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics

sourceReferences.title: Living Doc Stage Skill Operating Model
Living Doc Knowledge Graph, MCP, and Skill Model
Living Doc Code Representation Value Model

objectiveFacets.name: Target architecture
objectiveFacets.description: Typed definitions become the executable source for reusable semantic machinery while concrete living doc instances remain JSON artifacts.

objectiveFacets.name: Migration path
objectiveFacets.description: The refactor lands through reversible implementation steps that begin with one template family and expand only after proof.

objectiveFacets.name: Behavioral proof
objectiveFacets.description: Generated diagnostics must prove they add relationship or stage insight beyond the current hand-coded local section diagnostics.

objectiveFacets.name: Drift control
objectiveFacets.description: Generated artifacts, MCP behavior, rendered docs, and skill context stay aligned through deterministic generation and contract tests.

objectiveFacets.name: Decision trace
objectiveFacets.description: Settled architecture decisions and implementation constraints remain explicit while the refactor moves from additive graph metadata toward broader generated semantic machinery.

invariants.name: Concrete docs stay JSON
invariants.statement: Do not move concrete living doc instance content into code; code may define semantic machinery and generate artifacts, but living docs remain portable JSON artifacts.

invariants.name: Prove before expanding
invariants.statement: Do not migrate broad registry or template authorship until one generated template graph improves MCP diagnostics beyond existing structure reflection.

invariants.name: Generated artifacts stay readable
invariants.statement: Generated JSON and docs must remain committed, readable, deterministic, and compatible with the compositor and GitHub Pages workflow.

invariants.name: Objective is settled
invariants.statement: Do not treat remaining implementation checkpoints as open direction decisions; the objective is full integration of typed semantic definitions as executable semantic machinery while concrete living docs stay JSON.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows the current stage of the refactor at a glance so the integration objective does not disappear behind detailed architecture, proof, and tooling sections.

callout.title: Objective is settled; completion is not
items: The full refactor objective is integration: typed definitions become the executable source for semantic machinery while concrete living docs remain JSON artifacts.

The remaining questions are implementation checkpoints, not direction decisions: what to migrate next, what to generate next, how skills consume the MCP output, and what proof closes the full refactor.

The current slices prove the substrate works, but the refactor is not finished until the selected registry/template semantics, MCP behavior, skill operation path, render/sync path, and drift tests are integrated as one maintained system.

sections.title: Formal Model
sections.rationale: Defines the target semantic model for the refactor: what becomes executable typed machinery, what remains generated artifact, and what remains concrete JSON document state.

data.name: Typed semantic definitions as source of machinery
data.definition: Reusable living-doc semantic machinery is authored as typed definitions for types, templates, expected relationships, stage signals, diagnostics, and operations; generated JSON/metadata remain the artifacts consumed by the compositor, renderer, MCP server, tests, and docs.
data.authorityScope: Registry/template semantics, template relationship graphs, MCP stage diagnostics, valid operations, generated prompt context, and generated diagrams.
notes: This model does not replace concrete living doc JSON instances.
The first definition source should be additive and prove value on one template family.
violations: Moving concrete doc content into code would break the artifact boundary.
Generating artifacts that are not deterministic, readable, or compatible with the compositor would fail the refactor objective.

data.name: Generated template relationship graph
data.definition: A generated graph captures template section order, expected type relationships, stage signals, and valid operations so MCP can reason over relationships rather than only local section shape.
data.authorityScope: Read-only MCP diagnostics and generated documentation for template relationship maps.
notes: The generated graph includes machine-readable stage signal conditions, not only explanatory `when` text.
MCP evaluates those conditions generically for current docs rather than hardcoding template-specific stage logic.
The model now covers three different families: surface delivery, proof/canonicality, and operations support.

sections.title: Model Assertions
sections.rationale: Keeps the core claims of the refactor explicit so implementation does not proceed on hidden assumptions about JSON, code representation, MCP value, or proof requirements.

data.name: The full integration objective is settled
notes: The refactor is not exploring whether typed definitions should become executable semantic machinery; that direction is already the objective.

The implementation questions are sequencing and proof questions: which type/template surface to migrate next, which generated artifacts become canonical next, how MCP and skills use the generated behavior, and which contracts prove the system is integrated.
Small implementation slices should be recorded as checkpoints toward this objective, not as separate strategies or endpoints.

data.name: Living doc instances remain JSON artifacts
notes: JSON is still the correct representation for stored living doc state because it is portable, inspectable, patchable, renderable, and archival.

data.name: Code defines behavior over living-doc state
notes: Stage signal behavior now lives in typed definitions as condition objects and is evaluated by MCP generically.
This is the first concrete step where code representation carries behavior, not only documentation or generated metadata.

data.name: MCP diagnostic benefit must be proven before broad migration
notes: The first experiment succeeds only if generated graph diagnostics identify relationship or stage gaps that current section-local diagnostics do not express clearly.

sections.title: Design-Code-Spec Flow
sections.rationale: Tracks the implementation path toward full integration, not a set of optional experiments; each card should show how the refactor moves closer to typed definitions becoming the maintained executable source.

data.name: Full refactor integration path
notes: The destination is full integration: typed definitions author the selected semantic structure and behavior, generated artifacts stay deterministic and readable, MCP consumes the generated semantics, skills can operate from the diagnostics, and JSON remains the document instance format.

Current completed checkpoints include typed definitions for three template families, generated graphs and diagrams, semantic context in rendered snapshots, relationship and stage diagnostics, repair operations, patch drafts, and contract coverage.

Remaining checkpoints include deciding the next generated artifact boundary, expanding typed coverage where the current templates still depend on hand-maintained JSON/prose, making skill workflows consume the MCP diagnostics and patch drafts, and closing the proof that render/sync/MCP/tests all share the same semantic source.
Do not stop after very small slices unless the slice clearly advances one of those remaining checkpoints.

data.name: First PR: additive surface-delivery template graph
notes: Initial implementation slice landed: typed definitions for surface-delivery, generated template graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semanticGraph metadata in structure reflection.
Still partial because relationship-gap and stage-diagnostic tools are not implemented yet, and only one template family is covered.

data.name: Extend structure reflection with generated semantic graph
notes: `living_doc_structure_reflect` includes generated semantic graph metadata when the doc matches a known template graph.
Dedicated MCP tools now expose relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid operations for generated template graphs.
Still partial because diagnostics are conservative section/card-population checks; they do not yet inspect rich card-level edges.

sections.title: Code Anchors
sections.rationale: Pins the refactor to the concrete files that currently own registry, templates, MCP behavior, tests, and publishing so the migration stays source-grounded.

data.name: Current registry JSON
data.what_it_does: Defines convergence types, entity types, status sets, prompt guidance, source keys, text fields, and rendering metadata.
why_it_matters: This remains the committed artifact consumed by renderer, compositor, MCP, and GitHub Pages.
Typed definitions may eventually generate selected registry fields, but not in the first implementation step.

data.name: Surface delivery template
data.what_it_does: Provides the first candidate template family for generated relationship graph proof.
why_it_matters: The template has a clear expected chain: status -> design/code/spec flow -> alignment -> verification -> tooling.
That chain is currently described in docs and examples but not executable as generated semantic graph metadata.

data.name: Living doc MCP server
data.what_it_does: Exposes registry, objective, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools to inference-time agents.
why_it_matters: The MCP surface already has behavior; the refactor should feed it generated relationship/stage metadata rather than inventing a parallel tool surface.

data.name: Compositor embed sync
data.what_it_does: Embeds registry, templates, profiles, renderer, semantic context helper, and helper scripts into the standalone compositor and mirrored docs/scripts assets.
why_it_matters: Generated artifacts must preserve this publishing path; changing source authorship must not break rendered docs or GitHub Pages.

data.name: Typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the surface-delivery semantic graph source: section roles, expected relationships, card-level evidence rules, machine-readable stage signal conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first executable semantic definition that generates graph metadata instead of hand-maintaining that relationship model only in prose.
The flow-to-alignment relationship now carries a concrete evidence rule for shared card values.

data.name: Generated template graph artifact
data.what_it_does: Stores the generated semantic graph consumed by MCP and contract tests, including relationship evidence rules when definitions provide them.
why_it_matters: Keeps the generated relationship/stage/operation graph inspectable and committed while code remains the source.

data.name: Relationship and stage MCP tools
data.what_it_does: Exposes generated template graph and diagram metadata, compares living docs to expected section and card-level relationships, and returns relationship gaps, repair operations, likely stage candidates, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first point where typed semantic definitions produce inference-time behavior and inspectable representation rather than only a generated artifact.
Relationship diagnostics now distinguish populated-but-unlinked cards from genuinely connected cards where evidence rules exist.
Relationship gaps now include generated repair operations so skills can move directly from diagnosis to a bounded next action.
Relationship gaps now include minimal `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` drafts for direct card-create repairs where the target section exists.
Patch drafts include validation results from the existing patch validator so a skill can distinguish an immediately applicable draft from one that needs editing.

data.name: Proof-canonicality typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the proof-canonicality semantic graph source: model/assertion/proof/finding/decision/tooling relationships, assertion-to-proof evidence, stage conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This second family tests whether the typed semantic graph model generalizes beyond product surface delivery.
The assertion-to-proof relationship now checks whether proof rungs identify the claim, primitive, invariant, or authority they support.

data.name: Operations-support typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the operations-support semantic graph source: request flow, operating surface, enablers, tooling relationships, operation-to-surface evidence, stage conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This third family tests whether the typed semantic graph model covers support and mediated workflow docs, not only delivery and proof docs.
The operation-to-surface relationship now checks whether support lanes identify the operation, workflow path, or request input they support.

data.name: Semantic diagram generator
data.what_it_does: Generates Mermaid relationship diagram source from the generated semantic template graph.
why_it_matters: This is the first generated documentation projection from the typed semantic graph, reducing drift between runtime metadata and explanatory diagrams.

data.name: Semantic context helper
data.what_it_does: Centralizes semantic graph loading, diagram loading, template inference, semantic graph summaries, rendered semantic context construction, and rendered HTML semantic-context extraction.
why_it_matters: Renderer and MCP now share the same template-inference behavior instead of carrying duplicate docId/canonicalOrigin/section-sequence matching logic.
MCP can now retrieve semantic context from either source JSON or standalone rendered HTML.

data.name: Rendered semantic context embed
data.what_it_does: Embeds generated semantic graph and Mermaid diagram context into standalone rendered HTML when the document matches a known semantic template.
why_it_matters: Standalone snapshots now carry the generated relationship context needed by downstream tools, rather than relying only on repo-local generated JSON files.

sections.title: Attempt Log
sections.rationale: Records probes and migration attempts as they happen so learning accumulates without prematurely turning every experiment into a decision or reusable template.

data.name: Externalize the operating model into three internal pages
what_tried: Created detailed static pages for stage-aware skills, graph/MCP substrate, and code representation value.
what_proved: The concept can be explained coherently enough to seed a refactor issue and this one-off living doc.
notes: This is a thinking artifact, not proof that the implementation path works.

data.name: Capture refactor as GitHub issue #163
what_tried: Created a phased refactor ticket with first PR scope, MCP additions, generated graph artifact, and validation plan.
what_proved: There is a concrete enough implementation plan to track outside conversation.

data.name: Create one-off refactor living doc before making a template
what_tried: Represented the refactor with existing convergence types rather than adding a reusable refactor template prematurely.
what_proved: Pending: whether this doc shape stays useful during implementation.

data.name: Implement additive typed definition and template graph slice
what_tried: Added typed definition source for surface-delivery, generated graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semantic graph metadata in structure reflection.
what_proved: A typed definition can generate a deterministic relationship/stage/operation graph and expose it through the existing MCP surface without replacing living doc JSON artifacts.
notes: This proves additive integration, not the full relationship-gap diagnostic value yet.

data.name: Expose relationship gaps and stage diagnostics through MCP
what_tried: Added MCP tools for relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid stage operations over generated template graphs.
what_proved: The generated surface-delivery graph can drive inference-time diagnostics beyond raw graph lookup: a synthetic doc now triggers a Coherence-stage gap from flow to alignment.
notes: This is still structural diagnostics, not full semantic edge validation.

data.name: Move stage signal behavior into typed definitions
what_tried: Added condition objects to surface-delivery stage signals and changed MCP stage diagnostics to evaluate condition kinds generically.
what_proved: The typed definition layer can carry executable stage behavior rather than leaving MCP to hardcode template-specific stage logic.
notes: This condition language is deliberately small and should grow only when a second template family needs it.

data.name: Add proof-canonicality as second typed template family
what_tried: Added proof-canonicality typed template definition with relationships, stage signal conditions, and valid operations; regenerated the semantic graph and extended MCP contracts.
what_proved: The generated graph and condition evaluator are not limited to surface delivery; the same mechanism can diagnose assertion/proof gaps in a proof-bearing doc shape.
notes: This still uses structural card population as the evidence of relationship strength; card-level semantic edge validation remains future work.

data.name: Generate template relationship diagrams from semantic graph
what_tried: Added `scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-diagrams.mjs` and generated `scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json` from the semantic graph.
what_proved: The relationship graph can already generate Mermaid diagram source for the covered template families, reducing future hand-maintained diagram drift.
notes: The internal model page references the generated artifact but still keeps richer hand-authored explanatory diagrams for now.

data.name: Expose generated template diagrams through MCP
what_tried: Added `living_doc_template_diagrams` to the living-doc MCP server with direct template lookup and doc-shape inference.
what_proved: Generated relationship diagrams can be retrieved at inference time from the same semantic source as graph diagnostics, without asking a skill to reconstruct the shape from prose.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now checks surface-delivery and proof-canonicality diagram lookup.

data.name: Add operations-support as third typed template family
what_tried: Added operations-support typed template definition with request-flow, operating-surface, enabler, and tooling relationships; regenerated graph and diagram artifacts; extended contract coverage.
what_proved: The typed semantic graph model can carry support/workflow documents as well as delivery and proof documents, and the same MCP diagnostics can identify an operation-to-support-surface gap.
notes: This is still hand-authored typed definition coverage, not generated registry or template JSON replacement.

data.name: Embed generated semantic context in rendered snapshots
what_tried: Added `scripts/living-doc-semantic-context.mjs`, used it from both MCP and renderer, and embedded `doc-semantic-context` JSON in rendered HTML for matching template docs.
what_proved: Generated graph and diagram context can travel with standalone HTML snapshots, so semantic template knowledge is no longer available only through repo-local MCP reads.
notes: `tests/contract/render.spec.mjs` now renders a copied surface-delivery template and verifies the embedded semantic graph and diagram source.
This still embeds semantic context as a machine-readable script payload; no visible UI has been added for it yet.

data.name: Expose semantic context retrieval through MCP
what_tried: Added `living_doc_semantic_context` so inference-time tools can retrieve full graph and diagram context from either living doc JSON or rendered HTML snapshots.
what_proved: The embedded snapshot context is not just archival; it can be recovered through MCP and used as the same semantic reasoning substrate as source JSON.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now renders a copied surface-delivery template and reads its embedded semantic context back through MCP.

data.name: Add first card-level relationship evidence rule
what_tried: Added a `shared-field-value` evidence rule to the surface-delivery flow-to-alignment relationship and taught `living_doc_relationship_gaps` to report missing card evidence when both sections are populated but unlinked.
what_proved: Generated relationship diagnostics can now catch a real semantic gap beyond empty sections: an alignment card can exist while still not evidencing the surface-flow card it claims to judge.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now includes a surface-delivery document where flow and alignment are both populated but the alignment row points at the wrong Figma/code target.
This is intentionally one evidence kind before expanding the relationship language.

data.name: Extend card-level evidence rules across template families
what_tried: Added `shared-field-value` evidence rules to proof-canonicality assertion-to-proof and operations-support operation-to-surface relationships.
what_proved: The first card-level evidence mechanism is no longer surface-delivery-specific; the same generated rule shape can detect unlinked proof rungs and unlinked support lanes.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now includes populated-but-unlinked evidence-gap documents for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.

data.name: Attach repair operations to relationship gaps
what_tried: Added `repairOperationIds` to typed relationships and returned matching valid operation objects on MCP relationship gaps.
what_proved: Generated diagnostics can now be actionable: the same relationship definition that detects an evidence gap can tell a skill which operation should repair it.
notes: Surface-delivery suggests `add-alignment-row`; proof-canonicality suggests `add-proof-rung` or `weaken-assertion`; operations-support suggests `add-operating-surface-card`.

data.name: Add patch drafts to relationship gaps
what_tried: Added conservative `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` drafts to MCP relationship gaps when a card-create repair operation and target section are available.
what_proved: Generated diagnostics can now carry a concrete editable operation, not just a question and suggested operation label.
notes: The draft uses the relationship evidence rule to choose the target link field, such as `sourceCardIds`, `assertionIds`, or `operationIds`.
The draft is intentionally not auto-applied; it is a structured starting point for a skill or user to review.
The MCP response now includes the draft validation result from the same validator exposed by `living_doc_patch_validate`.

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Defines the safety and value proof needed to finish the full integration, so passing a narrow slice is treated as evidence toward completion rather than completion itself.

data.name: Completion proof spans the whole integration path
notes: The refactor is not complete just because one diagnostic, diagram, or patch draft works.

Completion requires proof that typed definitions are the maintained source for the selected semantic machinery, generated artifacts are deterministic, MCP behavior uses that generated source, skills can act on the output, rendered docs remain standalone, and contract tests catch drift.
Each implementation slice should name which part of this completion proof it advances.

data.name: Generated template graph is deterministic
notes: `node scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs --check` is covered by `tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs`.

data.name: Generated graph finds a relationship gap current reflection does not name
notes: `living_doc_relationship_gaps` detects missing or weak expected relationships from generated template graph metadata.
`living_doc_stage_diagnostics` derives stage candidates from machine-readable condition objects in the generated graph.
Contracts now prove section-level gaps for surface-delivery (`flow-feeds-alignment`), proof-canonicality (`assertion-requires-proof`), and operations-support (`operation-routes-surface`).
Contracts now also prove card-level evidence gaps for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support when target cards do not share the expected source identifiers or reference values.
Relationship gaps now include repair operations generated from the typed relationship definition.
Relationship gaps now include patch drafts with validation results for card-create repairs across surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support examples.

data.name: Existing contract and render tests remain green
notes: Targeted MCP contract passes with generated graph lookup, generated diagram lookup, relationship gap diagnostics, stage diagnostics, and valid stage operations across three template families.
Targeted render contract proves matching rendered docs embed `doc-semantic-context` with generated graph and diagram payloads.
`npm run test:contract` passes after adding generated repair operations to relationship gaps.
The targeted MCP contract passes after adding patch drafts, embedding validation results in the relationship gap response, and validating those drafts through `living_doc_patch_validate`.

data.name: Generated semantic context travels with rendered snapshots
notes: Rendered HTML for matching template docs now includes `<script type="application/json" id="doc-semantic-context">`.
The payload includes template inference method, generated graph template metadata, and generated Mermaid diagram source.
This keeps standalone snapshots compatible with tools that inspect HTML artifacts rather than source JSON in the repo.
`living_doc_semantic_context` can retrieve this payload from rendered HTML snapshots.

sections.title: Investigation Findings
sections.rationale: Separates observed facts about the current system from implementation claims and decisions, especially where the existing MCP surface already solves part of the problem.

data.name: The MCP surface is already richer than raw JSON reads
notes: `scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs` already exposes registry, structure, sources, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools.
The refactor should improve the existing MCP behavior with generated relationship/stage metadata rather than create a replacement surface.

data.name: The current gap is relational and stage awareness
notes: Current reflection checks sections locally and governance broadly, but template relationship chains and stage inference are not yet first-class generated machinery.

sections.title: Decision Record
sections.rationale: Preserves settled architecture constraints for the refactor. This section should not imply that the core objective is still open; it should distinguish fixed direction from implementation checkpoints.

data.name: The objective is full typed semantic integration
notes: The direction is settled: typed semantic definitions should become the executable source for living-doc semantic structure and behavior.
Remaining work should be described as implementation checkpoints and proof gates, not as open strategic choices.
A small slice is useful only when it advances the full integration path.

data.name: Do not move living doc instances out of JSON
notes: Typed code definitions are for semantic machinery; concrete living docs remain JSON artifacts.

data.name: Start with additive generated graph metadata
notes: The first PR should not rewrite the registry or existing template JSON.

data.name: Use surface-delivery as the first proof family unless implementation reveals a better fit
notes: Surface delivery has a clear relationship chain and should expose whether generated graph diagnostics add practical value.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.rationale: Tracks the generator, contract tests, MCP commands, and render/sync tools that must exist or remain stable for the refactor to be operational.

data.name: Semantic graph generator
data.purpose: Generate template relationship graph metadata from typed semantic definitions.
data.whenToUse: Run after changing typed definitions, and in contract tests to verify generated artifact determinism.
caveats: Covers surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support so far.
Generates additive graph metadata only; it does not generate registry or template JSON yet.
Relationship evidence currently supports the `shared-field-value` rule shape across surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.
Relationships can now name `repairOperationIds` that resolve to valid operation definitions in MCP gap output.
Relationship gaps can now produce minimal `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` draft repairs from the same evidence rule and operation metadata.
Stage conditions are intentionally small and structural: section-empty, related-relationship-gap, source-populated-target-empty, all-populated-no-high-gaps, and manual-review.

data.name: Template graph contract test
data.purpose: Ensure generated graph and diagram metadata is deterministic, references valid registry/template types and relationships, is exposed through MCP, travels with rendered matching docs, and can be retrieved from rendered snapshots.
data.whenToUse: Run as part of `npm run test:contract` after the generator exists.
data.invocation: node tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/template-diagrams.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/render.spec.mjs
caveats: Validates deterministic generation and registry/template references for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.
Validates relationship evidence rule shape in the generated graph.
Validates relationship repair operation references against each template's valid operations.
Validates rendered semantic context embedding on surface-delivery.
Validates MCP semantic context retrieval from both source JSON and rendered HTML.

Validates MCP relationship gap detection, repair operation output, patch draft shape, embedded patch validation, and patch draft validation for populated-but-unlinked surface/alignment, assertion/proof, and operation/surface cards.
Will need stronger assertions as generated diagnostics move from section/card population toward richer card-level semantic edges.

data.name: Existing living-doc MCP server
data.purpose: Expose living-doc registry, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render operations to inference-time agents.
data.whenToUse: Use as the integration point for generated relationship/stage metadata.
caveats: `living_doc_structure_reflect` includes semantic graph metadata for matching docs.
Relationship diagnostics validate shared-field card evidence where definitions provide it; broader edge semantics are still intentionally unsupported until needed.
